frameworks.name: topographic cortical maps
frameworks.one_line: Retinotopically organized parietal areas as units of analysis

frameworks.name: illusory shape processing
frameworks.one_line: Cortical responses to subjective contours absent in physical input

frameworks.name: dorsal vs ventral stream dissociation
frameworks.one_line: Parietal dorsal-stream involvement beyond ventral LOC for illusory form

frameworks.name: attentional modulation
frameworks.one_line: Task difficulty as a probe of attention-dependent responses

core_question: Do topographically organized posterior parietal areas respond to stationary illusory shapes, and how does attention modulate these responses?
key_assumptions: IPS subregions IPS0-IPS5 and SPL1 can be individually mapped with retinotopy
Inverted-inducer condition provides a valid non-illusory control
Fixation task difficulty parametrically manipulates available attention
manipulations: Illusory diamond vs lateralized triangle vs inverted (no-figure) inducers
Shape location: central diamond vs left or right hemifield triangle
Fixation task difficulty (easy vs hard) to vary attentional load
key_findings: All IPS areas (IPS0-IPS5) responded to illusory shapes versus non-illusory inducers
Posterior areas IPS0-IPS3 showed contralateral preference for lateralized shapes
Anterior areas IPS4 and IPS5 showed response attenuation with increased task difficulty
Functional dissociation between posterior spatial specificity and anterior attentional dependence
interpretations: IPS represents illusory content from stationary as well as moving stimuli
Posterior topographic maps carry spatial code while anterior maps depend on attention
